varieties.name: Essaouira Coastal Type
varieties.origin: Essaouira province, Morocco (coastal, oceanic climate) - northernmost locality with highest rainfall in argan range
varieties.traits: 8-10m height, gnarled thorny trunk, small lanceolate leaves 2-4cm, highest genetic diversity, coastal adaptation
varieties.yield_notes: Variable, lower than inland types but higher foliar antioxidants
varieties.availability: Seeds available via specialty importers (Daley's Fruit Trees, Trade Winds Fruit - Australia)

varieties.name: Agadir/Cha Maritime Type
varieties.origin: Agadir province, Morocco (near Souss River, maritime climate, shallow water table)
varieties.traits: Highest number of private alleles, strong genetic diversity, adapted to higher water availability than other types
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good in native range

varieties.name: Lakhssas Mountain Type
varieties.origin: Anti-Atlas Mountains, Morocco (mountainous, LOWEST mean annual precipitation of all argan regions)
varieties.traits: Most drought-tolerant ecotype, lowest genetic diversity but highest stress adaptation, extreme arid tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Lower but stable under drought stress

varieties.name: Aoulouz Inland Type
varieties.origin: Aoulouz region, upstream Souss Valley, Morocco (inland, lower RH, reduced water availability)
varieties.traits: Inland adaptation, intermediate drought tolerance, 50% polymorphism

varieties.name: Mazhar
varieties.origin: Morocco, domesticated selection for oil production
varieties.traits: Largest fruit size among domesticated varieties, selected for commercial oil production
varieties.yield_notes: Superior oil production in arid conditions

varieties.name: Naama
varieties.origin: Morocco, domesticated selection
varieties.traits: Pulp variety - highest energy value in pulp among varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Selected for pulp production rather than oil

varieties.name: Ghallate
varieties.origin: Morocco, domesticated selection
varieties.traits: Kernels and press cake richest in protein among varieties
varieties.yield_notes: High protein byproducts valuable for livestock feed

varieties.name: Khayr
varieties.origin: Morocco, domesticated selection
varieties.traits: Nutshell has highest moisture and protein content

varieties.name: Hamouch
varieties.origin: Morocco, domesticated selection
varieties.traits: Highest ash content in nutshell among varieties

varieties.name: Zakia
varieties.origin: Morocco, domesticated selection
varieties.traits: Balanced characteristics for general cultivation

varieties.name: Viveros Argania Type A (Cold-Resistant)
varieties.origin: Spain (Viveros Argania nursery selection)
varieties.traits: Most cold-resistant variety, slow maturation, very large fruits, more resistant and large leaves, lower flowering capacity

varieties.name: Viveros Argania Type B (High Production)
varieties.origin: Spain (Viveros Argania nursery selection)
varieties.traits: Less cold resistant, greater flowering and fruiting capacity, normal leaf size, characteristic reddish fruit color before full ripening
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity - ideal for commercial oil production in suitable climates

varieties.name: Viveros Argania Type C (Standard)
varieties.origin: Spain (Viveros Argania nursery selection)
varieties.traits: Normal size leaves, balanced characteristics

varieties.name: Taroudant Type
varieties.origin: Taroudant province, Morocco (Souss watershed, inland plains)
varieties.traits: Part of major argan production triangle (Essaouira-Agadir-Taroudant), inland adaptation, hot plains environment

varieties.name: Tiznit Southern Type
varieties.origin: Tiznit province, Morocco (southern coastal)
varieties.traits: Part of main argan biosphere reserve, southern range characteristics

varieties.name: Sidi Ifni Southeastern Type
varieties.origin: Sidi Ifni region, Morocco (southeastern coastal, Anti-Atlas foothills to coast)
varieties.traits: Southeastern range limit, adapted to coastal/mountain transitions

varieties.name: Safi Northern Type
varieties.origin: Safi province, Morocco (northwestern range)
varieties.traits: Northern range characteristics

varieties.name: Berkane Isolated Population
varieties.origin: Berkane region, northeastern Morocco (isolated outlying population)
varieties.traits: Relict population outside main range, unique genetic characteristics

varieties.name: Bouizakarne Type
varieties.origin: Bouizakarne region, Morocco
varieties.traits: Used in drought tolerance research

varieties.name: Negev Qetura Selection
varieties.origin: Qetura, Negev Desert, Israel (cultivated from Moroccan seed stock)
varieties.traits: 7th year height 4.0±0.6m, adapted to high summer temperatures, brackish water tolerance, desert conditions (hot days, cold nights)
varieties.yield_notes: Best yielders: 20-25kg dry fruit/tree (6-7 years), average 60-80kg annually vs 8-10kg wild Moroccan trees. Kernels 6.5% fruit weight, 55% oil content

varieties.name: Ramat Negev Selection
varieties.origin: Ramat Negev, Israel (good quality water, low winter temperatures)
varieties.traits: 7th year height 4.6±0.6m, cold-hardy selection
varieties.yield_notes: 20-25kg dry fruit/tree best yielders, similar to Qetura

varieties.name: Traditional Berber Landrace 🏆
varieties.origin: Southwestern Morocco, cultivated by Berber communities for centuries (indigenous to region since Tertiary period, 65 million years)
varieties.traits: Diverse population maintained through seed propagation, 150-200 year lifespan, deep roots to 35m, traditional agroforestry systems
varieties.availability: Seeds from Australian importers (Daley's, Trade Winds), direct Moroccan sources

varieties.name: Medieval Anti-Atlas Selection
varieties.origin: Anti-Atlas Mountains near Îgîlîz, Morocco (documented from medieval archaeological remains, pre-10th century)
varieties.traits: Mountain ecotype, traditional non-mechanized processing techniques still practiced in nearby Tifigit village (1280m altitude)
varieties.availability: Living examples in traditional villages, conservation programs
